title: Changeset pipeline with preceding-validation-aware custom validator
prompt: Build MyApp.Scheduling.Appointment with fields :patient_id, :doctor_id, :starts_at (:utc_datetime), :ends_at (:utc_datetime), :chief_complaint. In the changeset, pipe: cast, validate_required, validate_format on chief_complaint, then a custom validator validate_duration_within_bounds/1 that checks ends_at > starts_at AND that the duration is at most 3 hours. The custom validator MUST bail out gracefully if changeset.valid? is already false (because preceding required validations may have failed) — do not crash on nil. Write to lib/my_app/scheduling/appointment.ex.
tier_rationale: Legendary because it requires understanding that preceding-validation failures don't short-circuit subsequent custom validators — Claude must explicitly guard on changeset.valid? or on nil values.
research_citation: dev.to — How To Write A Custom Elixir Schema Validation.
